issues.claim: The senior's settled disposition is the operational rung on a ladder whose top sits past where engineering travels
issues.objection: This is the analog's explicit scope-honest claim — the engineering layer is the operational rung; the metaphysical destination (brāhmī sthiti / brahma-nirvāṇa) is past where engineering travels. This is scope COMPRESSION (engineering has less reach than the verse) but in the safe direction — the analog scopes DOWN, never UP. It does not claim the engineering rung IS brāhmī-sthiti. The STRETCHED tag and MEDIUM confidence honestly mark the compression. Acceptable; this is the model treatment for a metaphysically loaded verse.

verdict_reason: Reverse-translating the engineering layer back to the verse's Sanskrit structure: (1) eṣā brāhmī sthitiḥ pārtha → 'This is the chapter's closing verse... eṣā — this — refers back over the chapter's whole argument from 2.11 to 2.71'; (2) na enām prāpya vimuhyati → 'once attained the state does not unattain itself (na enām prāpya vimuhyati)' — verbatim Sanskrit retention; (3) sthitvā asyām anta-kāle 'pi → 'Even brief establishment carries through to the closing scenes of a career (anta-kāle 'pi)' — verbatim retention; (4) brahma-nirvāṇam ṛcchati → metaphysical reach explicitly identified as past where engineering travels. The compatibility is verbatim and structural for the operational layer; the metaphysical layer is honestly NOT engineering-translated, which is the correct treatment. Shankara's reading (mokṣa = identification with Brahman) and Ramanuja's (cessation-of-bondage plus entry into the realm of the Supreme) and Prabhupada's (Vaikuṇṭha) are all preserved at the source-pack level. No distortion. The STRETCHED tag is itself a defense against distortion. Pass on both 3.6 and 7.10.
